title: Quiz: Detecting Barren Plateaus
description: Test your understanding of barren plateau detection methods

questions.question: What is the primary method for detecting barren plateaus?

options.text: Analyzing how gradient variance scales with system size
Measuring the final cost function value

questions.explanation: The primary detection method is fitting gradient variance to an exponential decay: if Var[grad] ~ b^{-n} with b > 1, the circuit has barren plateaus.

questions.question: When fitting log(Var) = -α·n + β, what does α > 0.5 indicate?

options.text: The circuit has barren plateaus with significant exponential decay

questions.explanation: α > 0.5 means the variance decays faster than ~0.6^n, indicating significant barren plateau behavior where gradients vanish exponentially.

questions.question: What is the signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) in gradient analysis?

options.text: The ratio of circuit depth to width
The ratio of mean gradient magnitude to gradient standard deviation
The ratio of local to global cost
The ratio of training to validation error

questions.explanation: SNR = |mean(grad)| / std(grad) measures how distinguishable the gradient signal is from noise. Low SNR indicates difficult training.

questions.question: If a statistical t-test shows gradients are not significantly different from zero, the circuit is definitely untrainable.
questions.explanation: Non-significant gradients suggest difficulty but don't guarantee untrainability. Smart initialization, local costs, or other mitigations might still enable training.

questions.question: Why should you test multiple parameter positions when detecting barren plateaus?

options.text: Different parameters may have different gradient variances

questions.explanation: Parameters at different circuit positions (early vs late layers) may exhibit different gradient behaviors. Testing multiple positions gives a more complete picture.

questions.question: What R-squared value suggests a good fit for exponential decay detection?
questions.explanation: R² > 0.8 indicates that the exponential decay model explains most of the variance in the data, confirming systematic barren plateau behavior.

questions.question: What does examining the kurtosis of gradient distribution tell you?

options.text: Whether gradients follow a normal distribution

questions.explanation: Kurtosis measures how heavy-tailed the distribution is compared to normal. Barren plateau gradients typically approach normal distributions due to concentration of measure.

questions.question: Which diagnostic metrics indicate a trainable circuit? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: Large variance (a), high SNR (b), and small decay rate (c) all indicate trainability. Mean gradient of exactly 0 (d) suggests the cost function is symmetric, not trainability.
